answer:
\boxed{B}

The most common phobia in childhood is **nyctophobia** (fear of the dark), which often arises due to limited understanding of shadows or imaginary threats. Zoophobia (fear of animals) and claustrophobia (fear of enclosed spaces) are also common but less prevalent than fear of darkness in young children. Xenophobia (fear of strangers) is more associated with social anxiety rather than typical childhood fears.
